summary: Gruber promotes open sponsorship slots for both Daring Fireball and The Talk Show podcast in early 2023. He notes an unusual gap in the schedule, with the current week and several upcoming weeks open despite later months being sold out. He attributes the softness to post-holiday advertising budget cycles, though notes this year feels more uncertain than usual. He makes a pitch for the value of his sponsorship model, which has been running since 2007, emphasizing that repeat sponsors validate its effectiveness.
key_points: Daring Fireball's early 2023 sponsorship schedule has unusual gaps despite later months being sold out, reflecting advertiser skittishness
The weekly single-sponsor model has been DF's top revenue source since 2007, designed to satisfy readers and sponsors alike
Repeat sponsorships are cited as the strongest evidence the advertising model works
The Talk Show podcast offers similar sponsorship opportunities through Neat.fm
money_quotes: The new year is always a bit of a weird time, advertising-budget-wise, but this year seems a bit weirder and more skittish than most.
They've succeeded, I think, because they make everyone happy.

There's only one sponsor per week and the sponsors are always relevant to at least some sizable portion of the DF audience, so you, the reader, are never annoyed and hopefully often intrigued by them.
My favorite thing about them is how many sponsors return for subsequent weeks after seeing the results.
If you've got a product or service you think would be of interest to DF's audience of people obsessed with high quality and good design, get in touch.
key_insight: Gruber's brief sales pitch doubles as a window into the independent media advertising climate of early 2023, revealing advertiser caution while defending the durability of his single-sponsor-per-week model.
